Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/zerodds-bin.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-xbn85rp7...

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines sources as `.deb` packages hosted on GitHub Releases for both x86_64 and aarch64 architectures, all from the official repository `zero-objects/zero-dds`. These sources are precompiled binaries distributed via a legitimate upstream release channel. The `sha256sums` arrays use 'SKIP', which is acceptable here because the sources are version-pinned (referring to a specific release tag `v1.0.0-rc.3`) and the package is binary-only; skipping checksums for prebuilt artifacts is a common packaging trade-off when upstream does not provide signed checksums.

During `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare --printsrcinfo`, only the top-level PKGBUILD is sourced. There is no `pkgver()` function that dynamically computes the version (it is static: `pkgver=1.0.0_rc.3`), and no command substitutions or code execution occurs at the global scope. The `source` arrays list static HTTPS URLs; no `git` operations or dynamic fetching logic is present. The actual extraction and processing of `.deb` archives occur in the `package()` function, which is not executed during this phase due to `--noprepare` and `--nobuild`.

Thus, sourcing this PKGBUILD and downloading the sources poses no risk of remote code execution or data exfiltration during this narrow audit phase.
</details>
<summary>
No malicious execution during source fetch and parse
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No malicious execution during source fetch and parse[/]

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD contains no top-level command substitutions or executable code outside of function bodies. When sourced for `--printsrcinfo` or `--nobuild`, only variable assignments are evaluated — no arbitrary code runs.

All sources are fetched from `https://github.com/zero-objects/zero-dds/releases/download/v1.0.0-rc.3/`, which is the project's own upstream GitHub release page. This is entirely expected for a `-bin` package that repackages pre-built Debian binaries. There are no downloads from unrelated or suspicious hosts.

The use of `SKIP` for all checksums is a packaging hygiene concern (no integrity verification of the downloaded `.deb` files), but it is not evidence of malicious intent. All logic for extracting and installing the `.deb` files resides inside `package()`, which is out of scope for this narrow gate.
</details>
<summary>Standard -bin package; no malicious top-level code or suspicious sources.</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- Standard -bin package; no malicious top-level code or suspicious sources.[/]

data.reasoning: Both audits skipped due to source download failure. PKGBUILD is safe, but critical binaries were never examined.
coverage_issues: Critical gap: Both models failed to audit .deb binaries and any install hooks due to makepkg download failure
Unreviewed files: Actual executables/scripts in the .deb packages (primary security surface)
false_negatives: Potential risks in .deb binaries (network/filesystem access) were never examined
Possible malicious payload in extracted files during package() function execution
learnings: Tool must fail decisively when source download fails - 'skipped' isn't sufficient
Prioritize error diagnosis: Distinguish network issues from malicious source blocking
Add fallback mechanism: Allow manual source upload when automated download fails
Improve PKGBUILD pre-audit to detect source download risks (e.g. untrusted domains)
